name: 黄循财
current_positions: MP for Marsiling-Yew Tee GRC (Limbang ward)

education.institution: Harvard University (John F. Kennedy School of Government)

work_experience.position: Principal Private Secretary to PM Lee Hsien Loong
Minister of State for Defence and Minister of State for Education

work_experience.organization: Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY)

person_relationships.description: Co-chaired COVID-19 multi-ministerial committee; Gan serves as sole DPM in Wong's cabinet
Served as Principal Private Secretary to Lee (2005-2008); succeeded Lee as PM in 2024
Cabinet colleague; Teo succeeded Wong as Chair of PAP Community Foundation (2022)
Cabinet colleague; Desmond Lee succeeded Wong as Minister for National Development in 2020 and chairs PAP
Wong's career progression parallels Lee Kuan Yew's governance philosophy; part of the same PAP lineage

family_members.name: Loo Tze Lui
family_members.industry_or_organization: Wealth management; formerly a banker; served on YMCA Singapore board
Aerospace engineer at DSO National Laboratories

political_stances.stance_content: Announced GST increase from 7% to 9% progressively over 2023-2024, offset by $6.6b Assurance Package (later enhanced to $9.6b)
Stated mission to 'continue defying the odds and sustain this miracle called Singapore'; promised to 'never settle for the status quo'
Launched the 'Forward Singapore' movement, envisioning a society that 'benefits many, not a few'
Announced additional $1.1 billion Cost-of-Living Support Package for lower- to middle-income families
At National Day Rally 2025, outlined five key priorities: Economy, Young, Seniors, Future Plans, and Singapore Spirit

major_achievements.achievement: Introduced free museum entry policy for all Singaporeans and PRs to national museums and heritage institutions
Announced S$100 million National Youth Fund for youth to champion community and social causes
Led team to successfully bid for Singapore Botanic Gardens as UNESCO World Heritage Site
Co-chaired multi-ministerial COVID-19 committee, gaining national prominence
Selected as leader of PAP 4th generation team with 15/19 votes
Sworn in as 4th Prime Minister of Singapore; first PM born after independence
Led PAP to victory in GE2025 with 65.57% popular vote (87/97 seats); personally won 73.46% in Marsiling-Yew Tee GRC

biography_summary: Lawrence Wong Shyun Tsai (born 18 December 1972) is a Singaporean politician serving as the fourth Prime Minister of Singapore since 2024 and Minister for Finance since 2021. Born to a Hainanese Chinese family, he grew up in an HDB flat in Marine Parade. Educated at UW-Madison (BSc Economics), University of Michigan (MA Applied Economics), and Harvard Kennedy School (MPA), he began his career as an economist at MTI during the 1997 Asian financial crisis. He served as Principal Private Secretary to PM Lee Hsien Loong (2005-2008) and CEO of the Energy Market Authority (2009-2011) before entering politics in 2011. He held various ministerial portfolios: MCCY (2012-2015), MND (2015-2020), MOE (2020-2021), and MOF (2021-present). He gained prominence as co-chair of the COVID-19 multi-ministerial committee and was selected as PAP's 4th generation leader in April 2022. He became DPM in June 2022 and succeeded Lee as PM on 15 May 2024. He is married to Loo Tze Lui and has no children. He is a Methodist.
